items.title: Jewish professor says 'intimidating' protest changed his life on campus

children.content: Melbourne University professor Steven Prawer hopes the Royal Commission on Antisemitism and Social Cohesion will lead to campus change, following a 2024 demonstration that saw protesters invade his office.

articles.title: Jewish staff, students, describe enduring antisemitic jokes and Nazi salutes

children.content: Yasmine Johnson, who is central to the Free Palestine movement across Australian universities, has used her appearance at the royal commission to accuse antisemitism envoy Jillian Segal of "genocide denial".

image.altText: A woman with long dark hair in a dark blazer and white shirt stands behind a microphone looking off camera.

articles.title: Jewish academic fears being met by 'axe or gun' when on campus

children.content: Jewish academics and students say they do not feel safe on campuses, as the antisemitism royal commission prepares to hold a week of hearings scrutinising universities.

image.altText: University of Wollongong sign on glass wall.

gallery.altText: Man in blue shirt stands in front of office window and indoor plant

items.title: Former Victorian Young Australian of the Year charged over imitation guns

children.content: Darcy McGauley-Bartlett has been arrested and charged with a raft of offences, including possessing imitation firearms and police uniforms.

image.altText: man standing in suit holding award, looking proud

items.title: 'Frightening': Organised crime dominates the illicit tobacco market and it's now online

children.content: The process was simple and the service was speedy but the ease with which 7.30 acquired illicit tobacco products was concerning.

image.altText: Cigarettes on a table.

items.title: 'Devoted' Melbourne mother remembered by family after stabbing death

children.content: Lavanya Chappa has been remembered as a devoted mother and dear friend, one week after the mother-of-two was stabbed to death in her Melbourne home.

items.title: Geelong set star Cat timeline, Hawk sent for surgery

children.content: Geelong are hopeful star forward Jeremy Cameron will return in time to play a part in their premiership push after suffering a brutal shoulder injury.

image.altText: A Geelong AFL star grimaces in pain as he comes off the ground with a medical staffer during a game.

items.title: Proposal to cut blanket ban on climbing at Mount Arapiles Dyurrite

children.content: Climbing Victoria says proposed changes to the Mount Arapiles Dyurrite management plan will be a compromise between climbers and traditional owners built on "trust".

image.altText: Large rocks above Wimmera plains

items.title: Worn-down 'jewel' needs some love ahead of World Heritage Bid

children.content: Once a booming gold rush town, Walhalla is preparing for a different kind of boom, but first, it needs critical government investment in infrastructure.

image.altText: A small village of heritage buildings, surrounded by autumn trees, seen from up high

items.title: Melbourne cafe catches fire three days after alleged drive-by shooting

children.content: Police are treating a fire at an Essendon North cafe as suspicious, just days after the business was sprayed with bullets.

image.altText: Police tape seen at an Essendon North cafe.

items.title: Football homecoming brings 30yo tradition back to life

children.content: A special tribute match at Ensay's oval in regional Victoria resurrected a football tradition and brought generations of a Gippsland high country community together.

gallery.altText: A group of footy players pose in front of the score board after the game, smiling.

thumbnail.altText: A group of footy players pose in front of the score board after the game, smiling.

items.title: Homicide squad arrests man after dead body found in Gippsland home

children.content: Police arrest a 43-year-old man after a dead body was found in a home east of Melbourne.

image.altText: Police tape around a house at night

items.title: New exhibition challenges visitors to slow down and put their phones away

children.content: A new show at Hawthorn Arts Centre offers a range of immersive artworks made from books and paper that demand, and reward, our full attention.

image.altText: Palomita Soledad by Nadia Hernandez featuring large cutout letters adhered to a large canvas, hanging in a gallery.

items.title: 'Blown tyres, cracked rims': Victorian opposition's $5bn road repair pledge

children.content: The Liberals and Nationals are promising to make Victoria's roads a top priority if elected in November.

image.altText: A road with potholes filled with water.

items.title: Man charged after filming inside Dezi Freeman's hideout

children.content: A man who entered the property where Dezi Freeman had been hiding out in north-east Victoria has been charged with trespass.

image.altText: A heavily armoured police vehicle at the site of the Dezi Freeman shooting.

items.title: That's life: Derryn Hinch's most memorable media moments

children.content: A man of colourful contradictions, Derryn Hinch was a larger-than-life fixture on Australian radio and television over five decades who died on Friday morning at the age of 82.

articles.title: Derryn Hinch, 'human headline' and broadcaster, dies aged 82

children.content: Hinch was known for his distinctive broadcast style, his time in the Senate, and his law-breaking crusades to expose sex offenders that earned him several stints in jail.

image.altText: Hinch, dressed in a red scarf, talks to reporters outside Parliament House in Canberra

articles.title: Derryn Hinch remembered as 'tough old bugger', 'crusader for justice'

children.content: Media identity Derryn Hinch is being remembered as a provocative but courageous journalist who was ahead of his time in his fight against institutional sex abuse.

image.altText: A man smiles while posing in front of a long coastline and blue sky.

gallery.altText: Derryn Hinch on the TV show 'Hinch'
Daryl Somers in a white tuxedo with dancer Patrice Smith in a ballgown and Derryn Hinch wearing a tango frilly shirt

thumbnail.altText: Derryn Hinch on the TV show 'Hinch'
Daryl Somers in a white tuxedo with dancer Patrice Smith in a ballgown and Derryn Hinch wearing a tango frilly shirt

items.title: Teenage boy charged with murder of Victorian girl Layla Jeffery

children.content: The town of Donald in Victoria's west is in mourning, after remains believed to be missing 13-year-old Layla Jeffery were found by police on Thursday.

image.altText: A picture of a girl looking at the camera

items.title: Young country footballer 'alert' after serious on-field head knock

children.content: A young regional footballer who sustained a head knock and concussion during a match on Saturday is recovering well in a Melbourne hospital, according to his club.

items.title: Future of Melbourne's western football hub thrown into doubt

children.content: More than five years since a trumpeted public-private partnership to build a $150 million stadium in Melbourne's west, the soccer master plan has seemingly evaporated.

image.altText: An artist's impression of a packed stadium.

items.title: What falling home prices mean for affordability

children.content: Australian house prices have recorded their biggest monthly fall since 2022. For young buyers hoping it's a turning point, economists have some bad news.

items.title: Grandmother's 32-hour bus 'adventure' home after train shutdown

children.content: A bus trip that should have taken only 13 hours turned into a 32-hour interstate marathon for a Torquay grandmother due to this week's Telstra outage, while there is still no date for when Victorian passengers can claim compensation from V/Line.

image.altText: A woman with blond hair smiles with a regional bus behind her.

items.title: Mayor calls 'private prosecution' against him 'threat to democracy'

children.content: The mayor of Hepburn Shire in Victoria is the third council representative to be charged by the same resident via a rare "private prosecution".

items.title: Crocs, sharks and an Aussie cricket legend: All the action from the Barra Classic

children.content: Crocs, sharks and an Australian cricket legend have all turned up for one of Australia's biggest barra fishing tournaments on the Daly River.

items.title: $70k raffle to be redrawn over ineligible winner at Victorian sports club

items.title: Heading to the shed forges lifeline of friendship for isolated women

items.title: Regulator closes 45 family day care residences across Melbourne

children.content: For the first time, Victoria's Early Childhood Regulatory Authority has used its powers to suspend a childcare operator, closing 45 family day care residences across Melbourne.

image.altText: Kids toys on the floor.
